title: Should Students Have Longer Lunch Breaks?

steps.instruction: Write 2-3 sentences that introduce the topic and clearly state your opinion.
steps.work: Many students feel rushed during lunch. I believe lunch breaks should be longer because students need time to eat, relax, and be ready to learn.
steps.explanation: The introduction does two things: it tells the reader what the topic is (lunch breaks) and states your opinion clearly (they should be longer).

steps.subgoalLabel: Write a body paragraph with your first reason
steps.instruction: Write a paragraph that gives your first reason with supporting facts or examples.
steps.work: First, longer lunches give students time to eat a full meal. When lunch is too short, many kids throw away food because they run out of time. Eating a good lunch helps kids have energy for the rest of the day.
steps.explanation: This body paragraph has one clear reason ('time to eat a full meal') and supports it with a fact and an explanation. The transition 'First' signals this is reason number one.

steps.subgoalLabel: Write a body paragraph with your second reason
steps.instruction: Write another paragraph with a different reason. Use a transition to connect it.
steps.work: In addition, students need a break to recharge their brains. Studies show that breaks help people focus better. After a longer lunch, students can pay attention in class and do better work.
steps.explanation: 'In addition' links this paragraph to the first. A second reason makes the argument stronger. Supporting it with a fact ('studies show...') adds evidence.

steps.instruction: Write 2-3 sentences that restate your opinion and leave the reader thinking.
steps.work: For these reasons, schools should give students longer lunch breaks. When kids eat well and take a real break, they learn better all afternoon. Longer lunches are good for students and good for learning.
steps.explanation: The conclusion restates the opinion without copying the introduction word for word. It wraps up the reasons and gives the reader a final thought.

title: Should Schools Have More Art Classes?

steps.instruction: Introduce the topic and state your opinion in 2-3 sentences.
steps.work: Some schools are cutting art classes to add more time for math and reading. I think this is a mistake. Schools should have more art classes, not fewer.
steps.explanation: The introduction gives background (schools cutting art) and then clearly states the opinion (schools should have more art). The reader knows exactly where the writer stands.

steps.subgoalLabel: Write a body paragraph with your first reason
steps.instruction: Give your first reason with facts or examples to support it.
steps.work: For one thing, art helps students be creative. When students draw, paint, or build, they learn to think in new ways. Creativity is a skill they will use in every subject and in life.
steps.explanation: 'For one thing' introduces the first reason. The paragraph explains how art builds creativity and why that matters.

steps.subgoalLabel: Write a body paragraph with your second reason
steps.instruction: Give another reason. Use a transition word to connect it to the first paragraph.
steps.work: Furthermore, art class gives students who struggle with reading or math a chance to shine. Not every student learns the same way. In art, students can show what they know through pictures and projects instead of just words and numbers.
steps.explanation: 'Furthermore' connects this paragraph to the previous one. This reason shows that art helps a different group of students, which broadens the argument.

steps.instruction: Restate your opinion and leave the reader with a strong final thought.
steps.work: In conclusion, schools should offer more art classes. Art builds creativity and gives every student a way to succeed. Cutting art hurts students more than it helps them.
steps.explanation: The conclusion uses 'In conclusion' to signal the ending. It restates the opinion and summarizes the two reasons in a fresh way.
